items.name: W. R. Berkley Corporation
items.headline: EVP Lucille Sgaglione resigning, transitioning to Senior Advisor role
whatChanged: EVP Lucille Sgaglione resigning effective June 30, 2026, transitioning to part-time Senior Advisor role
Compensation to be adjusted commensurate with reduced Senior Advisor role
Outstanding long-term incentive awards to be treated per existing award agreement terms
No replacement named in filing; CFO Richard Baio signed the 8-K
items.decisionTakeaway: Departure of an EVP to a reduced part-time advisory role is modestly notable but does not signal strategic or financial disruption; monitor for any successor announcement.

items.name: Automatic Data Processing, Inc.
items.headline: ADP enters new $3.5B five-year credit agreement, refinances existing facilities
whatChanged: New $3.5B five-year revolving credit agreement dated June 26, 2026, maturing June 26, 2031, with JPMorgan as administrative agent
Facility split into US Tranche ($2.94B), Canadian Tranche ($325.7M), and Euro Tranche ($238M); proceeds for general corporate purposes
Refinances three prior credit agreements: 364-Day (June 2025), Five-Year (June 2024), and Five-Year (June 2025)
Benchmark rates: Term SOFR (USD), EURIBOR (EUR), and Term CORRA (CAD) with full fallback mechanics
Syndication led by JPMCB, BofA, BNP Paribas, Wells Fargo, and Deutsche Bank as joint lead arrangers
items.decisionTakeaway: Routine refinancing of revolving credit backstop with no impact to near-term earnings or capital structure; maintains ample liquidity capacity for ADP's $4.7B FCF base.

items.name: Ameren Corporation
items.headline: Ameren Missouri files $343M electric rate case with MoPSC
whatChanged: Ameren Missouri requested a $343M annual electric revenue increase from MoPSC, based on a 10.25% ROE, 52% equity capital structure, and $16.7B rate base
Rate case reflects increased T&D infrastructure investments, existing generation investments, and 400 MW of new generation resources
Request includes continued use of fuel adjustment clause and trackers (pension/OPEB, excess deferred income taxes, production/investment tax credits)
MoPSC decision expected by May 2027 with new rates effective June 2027; proceeding up to 11 months
Filing notes decreased costs from accelerating return of investment tax credits to customers
items.decisionTakeaway: The $343M rate case is a meaningful regulatory catalyst for Ameren Missouri's revenue trajectory, with approval not expected until mid-2027 and outcome uncertain given MoPSC discretion.

items.name: Harley-Davidson, Inc.
items.headline: Chief Legal Officer Krause departing; Littleton named replacement
whatChanged: Paul Krause ceasing as Chief Legal, Compliance and Corporate Affairs Officer and Corporate Secretary effective June 29, 2026
Gayle Littleton joining as new Chief Legal, Compliance and Corporate Affairs Officer and Corporate Secretary effective June 29, 2026
Krause to serve in advisory role until September 1, 2026, then exit with Severance Plan benefits
items.decisionTakeaway: Senior legal/compliance leadership turnover at a company already facing declining revenue and earnings warrants monitoring but is not a financial catalyst.

items.name: Iron Mountain Incorporated
items.headline: IRM prices $1.5B 6.250% senior notes due 2035
whatChanged: New $1.5B 6.250% Senior Notes due 2035 indenture dated June 26, 2026, with Computershare Trust Company as trustee.
Make-whole redemption at Treasury Rate + 50 bps through July 15, 2029, then par call — standard investment-grade-style terms.
Standard change-of-control put at 101% and investment-grade covenant release provisions included.
No new operational, segment, or guidance disclosures; filing is purely debt-document administrative.
Prior comparable 8-K (May 2026) covered annual-meeting voting results and committee appointments — unrelated to this debt issuance.
items.decisionTakeaway: IRM is adding $1.5B of 10-year unsecured debt at 6.250%, increasing leverage in an already highly levered capital structure (EV/EBITDA ~24x, negative FCF trend), but locking in long-dated financing.

items.name: Cboe Global Markets, Inc.
items.headline: CBOE amends and restates €1.5B Cboe Clear Europe facility
whatChanged: Cboe Clear Europe N.V. amended and restated its originally €1.5B revolving credit facility (originally dated July 2020), with CBOE Global Markets as guarantor
Royal Bank of Canada exited as bookrunner mandated lead arranger, revolving lender, and swingline lender
Continuing lender group includes 14 banks (e.g., Bank of America, Barclays, Goldman Sachs, J.P. Morgan, ICBC) with commitments adjusted via increase/reduction mechanics
Amendment effective date set for June 26, 2026, subject to conditions precedent including legal opinions and financial statements
Supplemental security agreements (English and Irish law) and amended collateral monitoring deed entered into alongside the facility amendment
items.decisionTakeaway: Routine annual amendment/restatement of Cboe Clear Europe's €1.5B clearing facility with one lender departure and commitment rebalancing—no material impact on CBOE's financial position or operations.

items.name: VeriSign, Inc.
items.headline: VRSN closes $550M 5.10% senior notes due 2031 indenture
whatChanged: Executed Third Supplemental Indenture for $550M 5.100% Senior Notes due July 15, 2031, issued at 99.961% of principal on June 26, 2026
Notes are redeemable at par one month before maturity (June 15, 2031); prior to that, redemption at greater of PV of remaining payments at Treasury Rate +15bps or 100% principal
Change of Control Repurchase Event triggers mandatory tender offer at 101% of principal plus accrued interest
Indenture adds Limitation on Liens and Sale-Leaseback covenants tied to Principal Property and Intellectual Property
Follows June 18 underwriting agreement; underwriters purchased at 99.361% vs 99.961% public issue price
items.decisionTakeaway: New $550M 5.1% senior notes modestly increase leverage but are easily covered by $807M annual FCF; no change to thesis on VRSN's high-margin registry cash-flow machine.

items.name: National Fuel Gas Company
items.headline: PUCO approves NFG's $2.62B Vectren Ohio acquisition
whatChanged: PUCO issued an order on June 24, 2026 accepting and approving NFG's $2.62B acquisition of Vectren Energy Delivery of Ohio from CenterPoint Energy Resources Corp, subject to certain conditions
Transaction expected to close in Q4 calendar 2026; will not close before October 1, 2026 without Seller's consent

Prior comparable 8-K (June 10) established $1.5B of acquisition financing notes: $500M 4.75% due 2029, $500M 5.05% due 2031, $500M 5.50% due 2036, all with special mandatory redemption if the CenterPoint acquisition fails to close
items.decisionTakeaway: Receipt of PUCO approval clears the key regulatory hurdle for NFG's transformative $2.62B Ohio LDC acquisition, materially de-risking the deal and the associated $1.5B debt issuance with closing expected in Q4 2026.

keyMetrics.acquisitionPrice: $2.62B for Vectren Energy Delivery of Ohio

items.name: The Kroger Co.
items.headline: Sargent transitions to Non-Executive Chairman; annual meeting results
whatChanged: Ronald Sargent ceases as employee effective July 1, 2026 but continues as Non-Executive Chairman; receives standard non-employee director cash retainer ($115,000) plus incentive shares (~$215,000) and an additional annual incentive share grant (~$250,000) for chairman role
Shareholders elected all ten director nominees; Sargent received the lowest 'for' votes at 468.97M vs 23.74M against, suggesting elevated but not dispositive dissent
Advisory say-on-pay approved with 438.06M for vs 54.63M against (~88.9% support); PwC ratified as auditor; Second Amended & Restated 2019 LTIP approved
Shareholder proposal for a report on GHG emissions reductions was rejected (85.78M for vs 404.19M against)
No changes to financial guidance, capital allocation, or buyback program disclosed in this filing
items.decisionTakeaway: Governance-only 8-K with no impact on fundamentals or guidance; the Sargent transition and shareholder votes are routine, though slightly elevated dissent on his re-election and say-on-pay warrant monitoring.

items.name: The AES Corporation
items.headline: AES Stockholders Approve $15/Share GIP-EQT Buyout
whatChanged: Stockholders voted ~97.92% in favor (67.17% of outstanding shares) of the $15.00/share cash acquisition by GIP (BlackRock) and EQT-led consortium
Deal valued at ~$10.7B equity / ~$33.4B enterprise value, including assumption of existing debt
Transaction expected to close late 2026 or early 2027, remaining subject to federal, state, and foreign regulatory approvals
Prior comparable 8-K (June 16) was an unrelated $1.0B senior notes underwriting agreement ($600M 5.200% due 2029; $400M 5.750% due 2033)
items.decisionTakeaway: With overwhelming shareholder approval secured, the key remaining risk to the $15.00/share takeout is regulatory clearance, making AES a near-pure merger-arb situation with a defined closing window of late 2026 to early 2027.

keyMetrics.voteInFavor: 97.92% of votes cast (67.17% of outstanding shares)

items.name: Visa Inc.
items.headline: Visa funds $250M into U.S. litigation escrow, dilutes Class B conversion rates
whatChanged: Visa authorized a $250M deposit into its U.S. litigation escrow account under the retrospective responsibility plan, effective June 25, 2026.
Class B-1 conversion rate decreased from 1.5475 to 1.5445; Class B-2 from 1.5075 to 1.5014; Class B-3 from 1.5075 to 1.4953.
As-converted Class B share counts reduced by ~749,815 total shares (B-1: ~6,658; B-2: ~2,973; B-3: ~740,184), having the same EPS effect as a Class A buyback.
Prior comparable 8-K (May 12) was a Makewhole Agreement exhibit related to Class B exchange offers—no overlap with this escrow funding event.
No changes to revenue, margins, guidance, M&A, management, or accounting; this is a routine litigation escrow funding under Visa's pre-existing retrospective responsibility plan.
items.decisionTakeaway: The $250M escrow deposit is a routine, pre-programmed litigation funding mechanism that modestly reduces share count and is EPS-accretive but immaterial to the investment thesis.

items.name: Robinhood Markets, Inc.
items.headline: Bazzano formally named principal accounting officer
whatChanged: Board appointed Dara Bazzano as principal accounting officer effective June 25, 2026; she has served as Chief Accounting Officer since April 2026
CFO Shiv Verma ceased serving as principal accounting officer but continues as CFO and principal financial officer
Bazzano's compensation unchanged: $425K base, 40% target bonus, $1.54M annual equity target, $3.3M RSU grant vesting over two years, $400K sign-on bonus
items.decisionTakeaway: Routine designation of an already-installed Chief Accounting Officer as principal accounting officer with no compensation change — operationally immaterial for investors.

items.name: The Kroger Co.
items.headline: Kroger Q1 EPS $1.46 (+13% YoY) as identical sales slow to 1.0%
whatChanged: Revenue rose 2.2% to $46.1B but identical sales ex-fuel decelerated sharply to 1.0% from 3.2% prior year, hit by Inflation Reduction Act (130 bps drag), brand-to-generic Rx shift (40 bps), and egg deflation (64 bps)

Diluted EPS $1.46 vs $1.29 (+13.2%); adjusted EPS $1.58 vs $1.49 (+6.0%) — adjusted FIFO operating profit up only 1.7% to $1.54B as FIFO gross margin ex-fuel fell 9 bps on higher transportation costs and price investments

Aggressive capital return: $213M buybacks (3.1M shares at avg $67.77) exhausted the $7.5B Dec-2024 program; $1.8B remains under new $2.0B Dec-2025 program; dividend raised to $0.35/share from $0.32 (+9.4%)
Debt reduced $571M QTD after repaying $500M of 3.5% senior notes; operating cash flow declined to $1.77B from $2.15B on less favorable inventory cash flows
eCommerce sales +13% (+19% ex fulfillment-center exits, Vitacost sale, and Ship Marketplace discontinuation); eCommerce including Media was profitable this quarter
items.decisionTakeaway: Q1 results show EPS growth driven primarily by buybacks and fuel margins rather than core grocery momentum, with identical sales deceleration and gross margin pressure signaling softer underlying demand that warrants caution on the forward outlook.

items.name: Stryker Corporation
items.headline: Stryker recasts segments, closes Inari deal, FY25 revenue +11.2%
whatChanged: New Ortho Tech segment created combining orthopaedic instruments with Mako/enabling technologies; all historical segment data recast — MedSurg & Neurotech grew 16.5% while Orthopaedics grew 5.4% in 2025
Inari Medical acquisition completed Feb 2025 for $4,810 net of cash; Vascular sales surged 50.6% and goodwill rose to $19,291M from $15,855M
Spinal Implants business sold to Viscogliosi Brothers in April 2025; Spinal Implants revenue collapsed 73.9% to $185M and 2024 included $977M in related goodwill/impairment charges
Interest expense jumped 48% to $607M from $409M after $3B in new senior unsecured notes issued Feb 2025; effective tax rate surged to 28.1% from 14.3% on IP transfers between tax jurisdictions
New tariff risk language added: US tariffs on imports from China and EU expected to increase product costs and adversely impact demand and supply chains
items.decisionTakeaway: FY25 results show strong organic growth and margin expansion ex-impairments, but the Inari-funded debt burden, elevated tax rate, and emerging tariff headwinds warrant scrutiny of whether adjusted EPS growth of 11.8% is sustainable into 2026.

keyMetrics.operatingMargin: 19.5% vs 16.3% prior year (impacted by lower impairments)

items.name: Carnival Corporation Ltd.
items.headline: Q2 revenue up 5% but operating income falls on fuel, ship-sale nonrecurrence
whatChanged: Q2 revenue $6.66B (+5.3% YoY) driven by higher ticket prices, 2% capacity growth, and favorable FX; onboard revenue +7.4% to $2.4B
Q2 operating income declined $83M to $851M as $121M higher fuel costs and $103M nonrecurrence of ship-sale gains offset revenue gains; 6M operating income roughly flat at $1.46B
6M net income attributable to CCL surged to $795M from $486M, with diluted EPS $0.57 vs $0.37, driven by $141M lower interest expense and absence of prior-year $255M debt-extinguishment costs

DLC Unification completed May 2026 — single listing on NYSE; $1.1B convertible notes settled in Dec 2025 via 69.1M shares + $500M cash; new $2.5B buyback authorized with $381M repurchased and $414M dividends paid in 6M
Total debt reduced to $25.6B from $27.4B at fiscal year-end; new data-security incident class actions (6 cases consolidated) and Havana Docks Supreme Court remand are new legal developments
items.decisionTakeaway: Deleveraging and capital-return initiation are positive structural shifts, but Q2 operating-margin compression from fuel and crew-cost headwinds warrants monitoring before chasing the post-unification re-rating.

items.name: VeriSign, Inc.
items.headline: VeriSign prices $550M 5.10% senior notes due 2031
whatChanged: New $550M senior unsecured notes priced at 5.100% due 2031, underwritten by J.P. Morgan, BofA, and U.S. Bancorp
Notes sold at 99.361% of principal (slight discount), closing June 26, 2026
Issued under existing indenture via Third Supplemental Indenture dated June 26, 2026
No prior comparable debt issuance in the prior 8-K (which covered equity plan amendments only)
items.decisionTakeaway: VeriSign is adding $550M of 5.10% fixed-rate debt through 2031, modestly increasing leverage but consistent with its strong FCF generation (~$1.1B FY25) and investment-grade profile.

items.name: Southern Copper Corporation
items.headline: SCCO secures $1.25B financing for Tía María; project details and Michiquillay update disclosed
whatChanged: Tía María project fully financed: $1.25B in 5.35% notes due 2036; total capex budget $1.8B with $948M already committed
Tía María production profile detailed: 120,000 tons/year refined copper via SX-EW, operations expected Q3 2027
Project economics disclosed: ~$24.1B in exports and $6.2B in taxes/royalties over first 20 years at current copper prices
Michiquillay project update: 225,000 tons/year copper, ~$2.5B investment, production expected 2032
5,300 jobs created at Tía María to date, with emphasis on local workforce hiring
items.decisionTakeaway: The filing confirms SCCO's growth pipeline is advancing on schedule with secured financing, adding ~120kt of incremental copper from Tía María by late 2027 and a longer-dated 225kt from Michiquillay by 2032.

items.name: Expand Energy Corporation
items.headline: VP Accounting & Controller Larson resigns; CFO Teunissen assumes PAO role interim
whatChanged: Gregory M. Larson resigns as VP – Accounting & Controller effective June 23, 2026; no disagreement cited.
CFO Marcel Teunissen (appointed April 6, 2026) assumes principal accounting officer duties on an interim basis pending a permanent replacement search.
items.decisionTakeaway: Routine controller departure with no disagreement disclosed and CFO covering interim PAO duties; no financial or operational impact indicated.
